info.name: RBPO Labs 2-3-4
info.description: Repeatable Postman workflow for the current RBPO Labs 2-4 API. Covers auth, role checks, licenses, certificate retrieval, and malware signature lifecycle.

request.description: Creates a fresh admin candidate for the current collection run. Promote this user to ROLE_ADMIN in the database before running the admin-only requests.
Creates a fresh regular user for the current collection run.
Logs in with the admin candidate credentials and stores admin JWT tokens.
Verifies that the promoted admin user can access the admin-only endpoint.
Logs in as the regular user and stores user JWT tokens.
Verifies that the regular user token works on the USER-or-ADMIN endpoint.
Refreshes the regular user JWT pair and replaces the stored user tokens.

exec: throw new Error('Run 05 Login as user first to capture userRefreshToken.');

request.description: Creates a license as ADMIN. Set collection variable ownerId to the registered USER id first because the current API does not expose user id in auth responses.

exec: throw new Error('Set collection variable ownerId to the registered USER id before creating a license.');

item.name: 14 Renew success (after manual DB ending_date tweak)

request.description: Before this request, run in psql: UPDATE license SET ending_date = NOW() + INTERVAL '3 days' WHERE code = '{{licenseCode}}';
Returns the PEM certificate used by the digital signature module.

item.name: Lab 2-3: Auth, Roles, Licenses, Digital Signature

request.description: Creates a new malware signature as an admin and stores signatureId plus the initial digital signature for later comparisons.
Reads the full signature export as a regular USER. The full export must contain only ACTUAL records.

exec: pm.test('Created signature is present in full export', function () {
pm.test('Full export contains only ACTUAL signatures', function () {

request.description: Fetches selected signatures by UUID as a USER.

exec: pm.test('Exactly one requested signature is returned', function () {

request.description: Updates the signature and stores the recalculated digital signature. signatureSince is captured before the mutation for increment checks.

exec: pm.test('Digital signature was recalculated on update', function () {

request.description: Returns stored previous versions of the signature after the first update.

exec: pm.test('History contains the pre-update version', function () {

request.description: Returns the audit trail after the update. The newest entry must describe the update and list changed fields.

exec: pm.test('Audit contains create and update events', function () {

request.description: Performs logical deletion. The record stays in the main table with status DELETED and must appear in increment responses.
Verifies that logically deleted signatures are excluded from the full export.

item.name: 23 Get all malware signatures after delete (USER)

exec: pm.test('Deleted signature is absent from full export', function () {

request.description: Verifies increment synchronization semantics: updatedAt > since must include the DELETED record.

exec: pm.test('Increment contains the deleted signature', function () {

request.description: After delete, history must still return the previous ACTUAL versions of the signature.

exec: pm.test('History contains update and delete snapshots', function () {

request.description: After delete, audit must include the delete event followed by the earlier update and create events.

exec: pm.test('Audit contains create, update, and delete events', function () {
